critical_blockers: COBIT-2019 (requires annual license for commercial use)
Gartner (prohibits AI training and commercial derivatives)
IDC (requires written permission for external use)
summary.recommendation: CONDITIONAL - WIDAI can proceed with commercial distribution ONLY if: (1) frameworks marked RED are excluded from derivative products, (2) attribution requirements are met for all frameworks, (3) each YELLOW framework is individually reviewed for specific use case, (4) ISO frameworks are licensed separately or excluded from detailed mappings, (5) legal review confirms fair use boundaries for research frameworks.

frameworks.name: COBIT-2019

frameworks.notes: COBIT is strictly protected IP. Commercial use, training delivery, or inclusion in WIDAI commercial products requires annual license from ISACA. Unauthorized commercial use is copyright infringement. Contact: IPinfo@isaca.org or +1-847-660-5557

frameworks.name: Gartner
frameworks.commercial_derivatives: No - explicitly prohibits AI training and commercial derivatives
frameworks.restrictions: CRITICAL: Gartner explicitly prohibits using content as input into AI/ML, creating derivative works, or using in commercial products. No scraping or automated access permitted. Commercial external use requires written permission from Gartner Content Compliance.
frameworks.widai_usage: Research analyst frameworks and magic quadrants referenced in role/competency mappings
frameworks.notes: MAJOR BLOCKER: Gartner explicitly prohibits creating derivatives or using content in AI systems. If WIDAI is derived from Gartner analysis or uses Gartner frameworks, it would violate their terms. Recommend excluding all Gartner mappings from commercial WIDAI or seeking explicit written permission from Gartner Content Compliance.

decision_gate.implication: WIDAI can be commercially distributed ONLY IF critical red frameworks are excluded or separately licensed. Current state shows 23 frameworks with RED (incompatible with commercial derivatives) and 28 with YELLOW (conditional/uncertain). Recommendation: (1) Exclude or obtain licenses for RED frameworks (SFIA, COBIT, Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, IDC, CMMI-DMM, all ISO standards, IEEE, HBR, DAMA, Deloitte, BCG, KPMG, WEF, CDO Magazine); (2) Conduct due diligence on all 28 YELLOW frameworks; (3) Prioritize clear GREEN sources (O*NET, BLS-SOC, NIST NICE/AI RMF, DCWF, EU AI Act, GDPR, Executive Order, FDA, FED SR 11-7, Evidence Act, DDaT, dbt Labs, Data Mesh, 80000 Hours); (4) For research frameworks (Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, IDC), evaluate whether fair use permits limited reference without creating derivatives; (5) Obtain written legal opinion on fair use boundaries for consulting frameworks.
next_steps: Identify which RED and YELLOW frameworks contribute the most critical role/KSA mappings
Prioritize licensing inquiries for highest-impact RED frameworks
Evaluate fair use defense for research/consulting frameworks vs. licensing approach

Plan commercial product strategy: (a) exclude restricted frameworks, (b) license key proprietary frameworks, or (c) create tiered product (free base with restricted frameworks, premium with full coverage)
Obtain independent legal review of derivative works boundaries
Document all licensing agreements before commercial launch
